description: Forge architecture constraint rules injected into every Agent system prompt

core.purpose: Business logic only. TanStack Query hooks, Zustand stores, pure functions.
core.test_requirement: Every file must have a .test.ts. Cover loading/success/error states.

ui.purpose: Pure UI components. No business logic, no API calls, no state management.
ui.test_requirement: Every component must have at least one Default Storybook story.

pages.purpose: Page assembly only. Connect core hooks with ui components.
pages.test_requirement: Playwright E2E for critical paths only.

domain.purpose: Business entities, pure functions, Repository interfaces. Zero framework dependency.
rules: Repository interfaces MUST be defined here, not in infra
Errors MUST use sentinel vars: var ErrNotFound = errors.New(...)
No DB calls, no HTTP calls — pure functions only
domain.test_requirement: 90% coverage. No mocks needed — pure functions.

infra.purpose: Repository implementations, DB access, external service clients.
rules: DB errors (e.g. pgx.ErrNoRows) MUST be converted to domain.Err* before returning
No business logic — only data access and mapping

api_handler.purpose: HTTP adapter layer. Parse request → call domain → serialize response.
rules: Handlers only hold domain.Repository interfaces, never concrete infra types
No business if/else logic in handlers
All domain error → HTTP status code mapping is in middleware/error.go ONLY
Handler files must not exceed 60 lines
api_handler.test_requirement: 80% coverage. Test normal + invalid inputs + error mapping.

rules: Only file allowed to import domain/, infra/, api/ simultaneously
No business logic — only construction and wiring

file_placement_rules.if: TanStack Query hook or API call

naming_conventions.go_constructors: New + TypeName, return interface not concrete (NewProjectRepo → ProjectRepository)

frontend_feature: core hook test file created (loading/success/error states)
ui component created if new component needed

forbidden_behaviors: Assuming requirements — mark ambiguities in spec.json clarifying_questions
Writing SQL or HTTP code in domain/
Importing infra/postgres concrete types in api/handler/
Using 'as' type assertion on API responses (use parseWithFallback)
